data.title: Fortnite returns to Google Play in US after court order
data.description: Epic Games' popular battle royale title Fortnite is back on the Google Play Store in the United States after Google complied with a U.S. District Court injunction, the company said on Thursday.

data.title: Morningstar maintains fair value estimate for Australia's Myer, expects margin gains
data.description:
** Morningstar maintains fair value estimate on Australia's Myer Holdings at A$0.83

** Clothing retailer on Thursday posted a 3% jump in total sales for first 19 weeks of 2025

** Investment research firm expects underlying EBIT margin for MYR to expand 180 basis points to a long-term level of 5.6% from fiscal 2028

** Morningstar says market fears margin erosion, but expects Myer to offset decline via store footprint optimization and online sales growth (up mid-single digits so far)

** Forecasts FY26 sales growth at 3%, tapering to 1% from FY27 amid structural decline in department store appeal

** Two of four analysts rate the stock "buy" or higher, two "hold"; their median PT is A$0.7, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Stock has fallen 62.4% this year, as of last close

data.title: Lego Billionaires Back $595M Rescue Bid for Struggling Vacuum Giant
data.description:
Freudenberg Group has stepped into the Nilfisk (NLFKF) story at a moment when the company had been struggling to rewrite its own. The German family-owned firm moved in with a 3.8-billion-kroner offer, proposing 140 kroner a share a 36% premium to Nilfisk's last close after an unsolicited approach triggered a full strategic review. What followed was a round of talks with multiple interested parties, and Nilfisk's board ended up recommending Freudenberg's bid after negotiating improved terms on one of the incoming offers. Kirkbi, the Lego family's investment arm and a 20% holder, is supporting the deal, bringing total backing to 50.9% with the caveat that their commitments fall away if a competing proposal arrives at least 10% above the current bid.

This offer represents a turning point for a company whose public-market journey has been defined by stalled momentum rather than expansion. Since its 2017 spinoff from NKT, Nilfisk's shares have lost about two-thirds of their value as the business wrestled with growth challenges while still signaling interest in making acquisitions of its own. Thursday's trading captured the sudden shift in sentiment: the stock surged toward 139 kroner at the open and was up 34% to 137.80 kroner by 9:40 a.m. in Copenhagen, hovering just shy of the takeover price. As Nordnet economist Per Hansen put it, investors may not be getting a triumphant finale, but they could be getting an outcome they can accept.

Freudenberg wants to close the deal in the first half of next year, contingent on receiving at least 90% plus one share and securing regulatory clearances. If successful, it plans to delist Nilfisk and move ahead with a compulsory acquisition of remaining shares a step that would formally end Nilfisk's time on the exchange. For shareholders watching a long-pressured company pivot into a new chapter, the bid could be the exit they had been waiting for, even if the ending looks different from what Nilfisk once hoped to engineer on its own.

data.title: GSK Says FDA Approved Oral Treatment for Gonorrhea
data.description:
By Mauro Orru

GSK said the Food and Drug Administration approved Blujepa as an oral treatment for uncomplicated urogenital gonorrhea.

The British pharmaceutical group on Thursday said the agency gave the green light for a supplemental new-drug application of Blujepa for adults and children 12 and older with limited or no alternative options for the treatment of gonorrhea.

Gonorrhea is a sexually transmitted infection caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae that affects both men and women and can lead to infertility and other sexual and reproductive-health complications. GSK said there is currently no licensed vaccine in the U.S. for the prevention of gonorrhea infections, with standard treatment relying on an injectable antibiotic.

GSK said the FDA's approval means Blujepa is now available to patients in the U.S. as an alternative, when appropriate.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com

data.title: JPMorgan Crashes India's Banking Party With Bold New Move
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase & Co. is leaning further into India's growth story, securing in-principle approval from the Reserve Bank of India to launch a new branch in Pune after almost ten years without an addition. The move could broaden the bank's ability to serve clients across trade finance, liquidity management and foreign-currency payments, building on a network currently limited to Mumbai, New Delhi and Bengaluru. Management's tone suggests rising confidence: Sjoerd Leenart, the bank's Asia-Pacific CEO, described India's domestic momentum and global integration as creating huge opportunities, a backdrop that continues to pull foreign lenders deeper into the country.

This is happening at a moment when global banks are circling India more aggressively, drawn by rapid economic expansion, strong credit appetite and intensifying corporate activity. JPMorgan has been expanding commercial banking locally to target mid-cap firms, while also scaling its global service centers and strengthening its technology and specialized-support footprint since opening a new campus in 2021. The approval also follows its last set of branch permissions in 2016, underscoring how selective its physical expansion has been compared with peers that have moved faster.

And that competitive contrast is becoming harder to ignore. Standard Chartered runs 100 branches across 42 cities, while HSBC (HSBC) won approval earlier this year to add 20 new branches as part of its wealth-driven India strategy. Unlike those firms, JPMorgan does not operate a retail franchise in the country, which could make the Pune opening a strategic step toward deeper corporate engagement rather than consumer banking. Still, the new branch possibly sets the stage for a broader push, especially as India remains one of the few major markets offering the kind of long-duration growth global banks have been chasing.

data.title: Lantronix Touches 52-Week High on Drone Collaboration With Trillium Engineering
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Shares of Lantronix hit their highest point in 52 weeks after the company said it was selected for a collaboration with Trillium Engineering that would expand its presence in the drone and defense markets.

Lantronix stock was up to $6.75 a share Thursday, and was recently 12% higher, at $6.52. The shares have risen 58% this year.

The Irvine, Calif., company said Trillium, which provides gimbaled-imaging systems for uncrewed-aircraft systems, selected Lantronix's edge artificial-intelligence solution and engineering services.

Lantronix's technology provides Trillium's products with AI object detection and tracking, image processing, precision targeting with low latency and size, weight and power optimization.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: US FDA expands use of GSK's Blujepa as treatment for gonorrhea
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said on Thursday it has expanded the use of GSK's Blujepa as an oral treatment for gonorrhea, a common sexually transmitted infection.

The health regulator approved the drug, chemically known as gepotidacin, as an oral option for the treatment of uncomplicated urogenital gonorrhea in patients 12 years of age and older who have limited or no alternative treatment options.

Gonorrhea is caused by a bacteria called neisseria gonorrhoeae that affects both men and women. If left untreated or inadequately treated, it can lead to infertility and other sexual and reproductive health complications.

There were more than 600,000 cases of gonorrhea reported in the United States in 2023 according to the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, making it the second most commonly reported sexually transmitted infection in the country.

It is treatable and curable with antibiotics. The current standard treatment is an injectable antibiotic.

"The ability of N. gonorrhoeae to develop resistance to currently available options, including standard of care, makes it important to expand the range of effective oral treatments," said Tony Wood, chief scientific officer at GSK.

Gepotidacin is already approved in the United States for women aged 12 years and older to treat uncomplicated UTIs, which are bacterial infections affecting the lower urinary tract.

GSK is banking on new drugs in its infectious diseases portfolio, including its recently launched respiratory syncytial virus vaccine, to make up for lost revenue from its best-selling medicines and looming patent losses for its HIV treatments.

The approval makes Blujepa the first in a new antibiotic class for the treatment of gonorrhoea approved in decades. It was based on results from a late-stage study that showed gepotidacin was not inferior to Roche's injectable ceftriaxone and Pfizer's oral azithromycin - a leading combination treatment regimen for gonorrhea.

data.title: PLATTS: 300--Platts Power US Eastern Daily Commentary - 11Dec25
data.description: (Platts)--11Dec2025/137 pm EST/1837 GMT Wholesale power prices in the Northeast for day-ahead delivery saw bullish activity on Dec. 11, as the National Weather Service issued wind advisories for New York and Boston. Prices in the New York Independent System Operator saw increases across both on-peak and off-peak packages. Zone J NYC climbed $6.75 to $147/MWh on its on-peak package, while rising around $19.50 to $112/MWh on its off-peak counterpart. Meanwhile, Zone G Hudson Valley on-peak price rose around $8 to $146.50/MWh, and its off-peak package increased about $20.25 to $112.25/MWh for delivery Dec. 12. On ISO-New England, Mass hub on-peak package rose around $9.25 to $163.25/MWh. Its off-peak counterpart drew offers at $164/MWh with a previous price of $150/MWh. Peakload demand across both Independent System Operators was anticipated to drop, with NYISO expecting a 2.91% fall to 21.15 GW and ISO-NE forecasts showing a 1.65% decrease to 17.85 GW Dec. 12, according to the grid operators. WIND ADVISORIES FOR NEW YORK, BOSTON Boston and NYC were expected to experience up to 30 mph and 20 mph winds, respectively, both with gusts up to 50 mph during Dec. 11-12, according to wind advisories issued by the National Weather Service Dec. 11. Meanwhile, temperatures were expected to decrease day over day on Dec. 12, with New York highs falling 6 F to 36 F (6 C) and Boston's dropping 7 to 34 F, 10 F and 9 F below their five-day norm, respectively, according to CustomWeather. In gas prices, Algonquin city-gates and Transco Zone 6 NY dropped $1.17 and $1.67 to around $19/MMBtu and $6.83/MMBtu, respectively, for day-ahead delivery. Platts is part of S&P Global Energy. — Mauricio Avila, mauricio.avila@spglobal.com-- Edited by Ashanti Rojano, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Blackstone Just Bought the Risk--ABN Amro Unlocks 1.6B in Lending Ammo
data.description:
ABN Amro's (ABNRY) latest move to reshape its balance sheet lands at a moment when banks and private-capital giants are leaning harder into risk-transfer structures that could create room for fresh lending. The Dutch lender completed a new deal with Blackstone , handing over first-loss protection on a 2 billion portfolio of large corporate loans. For ABN Amro, the shift could release 1.6 billion in risk-weighted assets, giving the bank space to reallocate capital in pursuit of higher-return areas. For Blackstone, the agreement offers diversified exposure to ABN Amro's corporate-client book at a time when investor interest in structured risk-sharing has been building.

Inside the bank, this deal sits squarely within a broader effort to reposition up to 8 billion in risk-weighted assets toward businesses that could produce stronger long-term returns. Dan Dorner, Chief Commercial Officer for ABN Amro's corporate-banking arm, said the partnership with Blackstone could help the lender push deeper into sectors and products where it sees better profitability potential. It also underscores how private-capital firms continue to step into the credit ecosystem as banks respond to regulatory pressures and evolving client demand.

This latest transaction follows an earlier risk-sharing arrangement tied to a 1 billion Dutch loan portfolio announced with the EIB Group earlier this year. The market around these significant risk-transfer structures has been picking up speed, with Bloomberg Intelligence estimating that global issuance could grow by about 11% annually over the next two years. For investors, ABN Amro's deeper push into SRTs may signal how lenders could increasingly partner with private-market capital to build flexibility, manage constraints, and position themselves for opportunities that may emerge in the next phase of credit growth.
